steps.title: Identify the key comparison
steps.content: The passage sets up two things: the standard view (liberalization would help companies raise money) and Henry's empirical finding (companies in 11 liberalizing countries did receive significant investment increases). The blank should explain what this finding reveals about the standard view.

steps.title: Apply the finding to the choices
steps.content: Choice D correctly states that empirical evidence was consistent with the scholarly consensus—since Henry found investment increases following liberalization, the actual outcome matched the prediction. Choices A and C introduce unsupported details about specific countries or timing, while B falsely claims the standard view failed to anticipate effects. That matches choice D — D is correct.
